name: Potential Mailto Ransomware Behavior (Windows)
notes: This rule triggers when a potential mailto ransomware behavior is detected. A mailto ransomware typically performs the following steps (may have slight changes based on the variant): 1. Registry changes to create keys (for persistence). 2. Drops executable in temporary directory. 3. Deletes shadow copies. 4. Encryption (file modification). This rule detects the behavior of the ransomware.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Excessive File Modifications match at least 1 times with the same Machine Identifier (custom) in 1 hour(s) after BB:BehaviorDefinition: New Registry Added to HKLM/HKCU (Windows) match
BB:BehaviorDefinition: New File Created in Temporary Directory
BB:BehaviorDefinition: New Registry Added to HKLM/HKCU (Windows)
conditions_text: when Attempt to Delete Shadow Copies, BB:BehaviorDefinition: New File Created in Temporary Directory, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Excessive File Modifications match at least 1 times with the same Machine Identifier (custom) in 1 hour(s) after BB:BehaviorDefinition: New Registry Added to HKLM/HKCU (Windows) match
thresholds: when Attempt to Delete Shadow Copies, BB:BehaviorDefinition: New File Created in Temporary Directory, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Excessive File Modifications match at least 1 times with the same Machine Identifier (custom) in 1 hour(s) after BB:BehaviorDefinition: New Registry Added to HKLM/HKCU (Windows) match
TD_list: APPLY Potential Mailto Ransomware Behavior (Windows) on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when Attempt to Delete Shadow Copies, BB:BehaviorDefinition: New File Created in Temporary Directory, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Excessive File Modifications match at least 1 times with the same Machine Identifier (custom) in 1 hour(s) after BB:BehaviorDefinition: New Registry Added to HKLM/HKCU (Windows) match
RED_list: Event name: Potential Mailto Ransomware Behaviour (Windows)
Event description: Detects a potential mailto ransomware behavior.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
